title: Auto-distill fallback and kage resume continuity
summary: kage distill accepts auto: it writes distilled session candidates to the pending inbox .agent memory/pending, status "pending", tag "auto distill", quality.distillation "auto distill" instead of approved memory, and quie
body: kage distill accepts --auto: it writes distilled session candidates to the pending inbox (.agent_memory/pending, status "pending", tag "auto-distill", quality.distillation "auto_distill") instead of approved memory, and quietly skips sessions with no observations or sessions where the agent already captured memory (sessionAlreadyCaptured in mcp/kernel.ts ignores mechanical packets: repo_map/kage-indexer generated, diff-proposal, change-memory, auto-distill). Auto-distilled pending drafts are excluded from recall entirely (recallablePendingPackets filters the auto-distill tag). kage resume prints a <=15-line "previously" digest (last session, distilled titles, change memory, pending auto-distill count, reconciliation items) and prints nothing when there is no prior session data. The claude-code Stop hook calls distill --auto after refresh/reconcile and the SessionStart hook appends kage resume output to the injected policy; both hook scripts exist in TWO copies that must stay in sync: the templates in setupAgent (mcp/kernel.ts) and the vendored plugin/hooks/stop.sh + session-start.sh.

The claude-code Stop hook calls distill --auto after refresh/reconcile and the SessionStart hook appends kage resume output to the injected policy; both hook scripts exist in TWO copies that must stay in sync: the templates in setupAgent (mcp/kernel.ts) and the vendored plugin/hooks/stop.sh + session-start.sh.
